>> Hi. Hi everyone. I'm Tina. Nice to meet
you.
>> Can you please introduce yourself?
>> Hi, I'm Tina. I'm very happy to be here.
I love Kelly. [laughter]
I love you. So, I've been teaching

>> That speaks volumes about you as a

People now know your energy and that's

certain things are irreplaceable. like

>> The videos are very inspirational
because they're not just there to teach

with you, build healthy English habits.
I watched your content. You are in a
situation and then you teach English
that applies directly to what you're

think that can help students learning
English. Learning English is all about
like real life training. What eventually

Every day all the conversations they had
inspired me to learn English. It was not

from the textbook. It was like wow
people actually say that. That's what
led me to improve my English and at the

your English learning journey was with
Oprah Winfrey. Yes. She doesn't know
that I exist. [laughter]
>> Hi Oprah. Oprah, please.
>> That is truly natural English. Even
though you get good grades on TOEIC and

speak with native speakers. So you got
to have practice. Did you ever study
abroad? Tell me about your English
learning journey. I studied abroad, you
know, short period of time. So, I would
say like seven and a half months in

You've only spent seven months abroad.
So, you've done the majority of your
English learning right here in Korea.
>> What's your advice for people doing the

how can they improve? Watch Callie's

>> I used to talk to myself in English a
lot. If that becomes your habit, then
when you are in the real life situation,
you just naturally speak English in that
way. But for some people, they could
feel awkward. I feel like I'm making a
lot of grammatical mistakes. That's what
I'm talking about. You need to make as
many grammatical errors as possible.
Those experiences can be a really firm
foundation for you to know that, oh
yeah, it's okay to make mistakes, right?
I'm just a human being who enjoys
speaking English. That growth mindset
really helps you to learn English, to go
the extra mile in whatever you do. I'm
not speaking perfectly 100% of the time.
Natural everyday English is not 100%
grammatically correct. My students have
been asking me, "How can I speak like
Kyo? How can I speak like American?"
Don't try to be Kyo because you're
Korean. Students have like high
standard. That high standard can
sometimes make you feel frustrated. I
always say English is not just about
learning it for 10 years or 5 years.
It's like a lifelong journey. I'm guilty
of this because I am often a
perfectionist. I get afraid of making
mistakes. I do. Perfectionism is such a
hindrance when you want to get better at
anything in life. As you know, there are
so many students in my class from
different like backgrounds and they have
different jobs and they come to my class
and tell me, "Oh, my English is not
good." First is perfectionism and second
is competition. This life is not about
competition. This life is also about
teamwork. When it comes to learning
English, it's important to find a good
teacher, a good learning partner. How
has learning English changed your life?
Everything. I didn't have much courage
to initiate conversation with other
people. Thanks to this learning process,
I got to like start a conversation with
strangers. I could open up more about my
personal life. That's how I got to
connect with my students and I relate to
what my students are going through. I
don't mainly focus on study this, you
got to do your homework. I first listen
to my students about how they feel in
their lives these days and I ask them
how they have been learning English.
That's why every class that I have feels
very different. It's kind of
personalized for each person. Have you
had any experiences with some of your
students where they opened up to you
about how English changed their life
every day? Yeah, [laughter] every day
one of my students, she's a pediatrician
and she texted me, Tina, like there are
patients from other countries and in the
past I would use my translator, but
these days I just naturally speak
English and can make jokes and interact
with kids. So she said, "Wow, this is
such a wonderful experience." There are
a lot of students who have children and
they tell me when their kids ask the
questions, they would go like, "Ask your
dad. I'm going to look it up." But now
they answer, they give their kids
answers in English. What is a Tina Coup
pro tip for learning English? Find the
one that you enjoy the most. If you're
in a medical field, start watching
medical TV series. What I did in the
past is I always memorize at least three
sentences every day and repeat the same
sentences every day.
>> Don't just do it three times or five
times. You got to do it every day. If
it's too much, just one per each day so
that you can build healthy habits. So
when students ask me about good tips, I
ask them, "What are the English
sentences that you say these days?"
>> Hi, good evening. Have a great day.
Thank you. [laughter]
Thank you. I'm like, "No, no, no. More
than that. More than those." And they're
like, "I can't remember any of the
sentences." Now, then that's where
you're going to have to start. Even
though you watch a lot of videos in
English and you study with uh English
books, it's about what you can say out
loud. When you watch like English videos
for an hour and you feel like, "Oh yeah,
I studied English." Mm- [laughter] not
yet. You got to think how many
expressions remain in your mind. So for
basic level students, they should repeat
the same three sentences for a week. But
for intermediate students, they can do
more. Do not memorize just words. For
example, I'm rooting for you. You should
memorize words chunk by chunk. Root for.
And I help my students visualize the
structure itself. So rude for I'm
rooting for you. students can think of
those expressions whenever they want to
cheer people up. What I focus on is how
to make the most of what you know and
what you already learned. I encourage my
students to review what they learn. If
it's too much, just one per each day.
But what I mean is you got to say those
sentences with confidence. So there are
three steps. First, how many sentences
do you know? Can you make the most of
what you learn? Can you say those
sentences confidently? You don't have to
see the whole staircase. Just take the
first step.
>> This is what Martin Luther King said.
>> Just take the first step and this step
will lead you to the next step and the
next step. So that's how you build good
habits and at the same time that's how
you improve your English.
>> That's true. You're full of quotes. I
